summary: Iteration 3 backend tests: all 29 pytest cases PASSED. Verified new 'direction' field, new status enum (running|delayed|arriving|cancelled), ETA penalties (delayed +8, cancelled +30, arriving 0), seed defaults (status='running', direction=null), bus ordering, SocketIO bus_added & bus_location events on /api/socket.io still work post schema change. No 'on_time' present in seed.

minor.issue: Duplicate stop_ids in stops[] (e.g. stops=[A,A]) are incorrectly rejected with 400 'one or more stops not found'. Root cause: validator compares len(SELECT ... WHERE stop_id IN (?,?)) (deduped by SQLite to 1 row) against len(stops)=2. Fix: validate with set(stops) instead — e.g. assert set(found_ids) >= set(stops). Documented in test_create_bus_same_source_dest_stops_documented_behaviour.

Status field is NOT validated against the enum {running, delayed, arriving, cancelled}. Any arbitrary string is accepted and persisted (e.g. status='foo' would store as-is). Consider adding server-side enum validation to match frontend constants in /app/frontend/src/lib/status.js.
status param also unvalidated — same enum-mismatch risk.

action_items: Optional: fix duplicate-stop_id validator bug in POST /api/buses (use set comparison).
Optional: add server-side status enum validation on POST /api/buses and POST /api/buses/{id}/location.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py create_bus stop-existence check uses `len(found) != len(stops)` which fails for duplicate stop_ids — replace with `set(found_ids) != set(stops)` so [A,A] is acceptable.

No enum guard for `status` on bus create or location update — backend silently accepts arbitrary strings; ETA penalty branches only match exact strings 'delayed'/'cancelled', anything else (typos, unexpected values) gets the base ETA with no signal.
Direction column normalization is good (empty/whitespace -> NULL via `(body.get('direction') or '').strip() or None`).
Test suite is session-scoped; persistent SQLite means TEST_* buses accumulate across runs. Consider a session-scope teardown that deletes WHERE number LIKE 'TEST_%' for hygiene (not blocking).
test_credentials: n/a — no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_* prefixed buses/stops (TEST_QA_Stop, TEST_X1, TEST_DIR_NULL, TEST_DIR_EMPTY, TEST_ST_{enum}, TEST_SAME_SD, TEST_ETA_{enum}, TEST_SOCK1). These persist in transitpulse.db across runs but the seeded-buses test was scoped to {M15,B25,M5,Q44} so re-runs are stable.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py is the canonical regression suite. Run via `cd /app/backend && python -m pytest tests/backend_test.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml`. Two known validator gaps (duplicate stop_ids rejected, no status enum guard) are documented as minor issues — main agent should decide whether to harden. Seed buses now use status='running' (legacy 'on_time' is gone). SocketIO path /api/socket.io confirmed working with websocket transport.
rca of the issue: Duplicate stop_id RCA: `SELECT stop_id FROM stops WHERE stop_id IN (?,?)` with both bind params equal collapses to one row in result set. The validator then sees len(rows)=1 != len(stops)=2 and returns 400 'one or more stops not found' — misleading message because the stop DOES exist. Reproduction: POST /api/buses with {number:'X',name:'Y',stops:[<valid_sid>, <valid_sid>]}. Mitigation: replace existence check with `set(r['stop_id'] for r in found) >= set(stops)`.
